items.title: Prosecutors ask a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for Regeni murder
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 23 - Rome prosecutors on Tuesday requested a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for four Egyptian security officials in their in-absentia trial in Rome for torturing to death Italian student Guiulio Regeni in January-February 2016. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, a week after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. Regeni, a Friuli-born Cambridge University doctoral researcher into Cairo street unions, was allegedly abducted and tortured to death by National Security Genera
items.titleIt: Prosecutors ask a life term and three 17-and-a-half-year terms for Regeni murder

items.title: Egypt chose to protect Regeni's killers says Rome prosecutor
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 23 - Egypt chose to protect the men who tortured Giulio Regeni to death rather than make them face justice, Rome Deputy Chief Prosecutor Sergio Colaiocco said on Tuesday in his closing arguments in the in-absentia trial of four members of the Egyptian security services for the homicide. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, a week after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. "A body shattered by pain," Colaiocco said as he showed images of a CAT scan of Regeni's corpse. "This is precisely
items.titleIt: Egypt chose to protect Regeni's killers says Rome prosecutor

items.title: Italian Cooperation Supports Surgical Services at Cairo Hospital
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 23 GIU - Italian Cooperation, together with the University of Molise and the Egyptian Ministry of Health, is supporting the improvement of surgical services at the Umberto I Hospital in Cairo. The Italian hospital is considered a model of excellence in healthcare for all of Egypt. The goal is to modernize the operating rooms and strengthen the skills of healthcare professionals. Read article...
items.titleIt: Italian Cooperation Supports Surgical Services at Cairo Hospital

items.title: Somalia: U.S. Resumes Air Strikes in Somalia After Brief Lull, Africom Says
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu, June 23 -- The United States has resumed air strikes in Somalia after a brief pause, carrying out a series of operations against Al-Shabaab militants in coordination with the Somali government, U.S. military officials said.
items.titleIt: Somalia: U.S. Resumes Air Strikes in Somalia After Brief Lull, Africom Says

items.title: Regeni murdered by 'cold, methodological violence' - Rome prosecutor
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 23 - Giulio Regeni was tortured to death by the use of "cold, methodological, organized violence against defenceless man," 'Rome Deputy Chief Prosecutor Sergio Colaiocco said on Tuesday in his closing arguments in the in-absentia trial of four members of the Egyptian security services. The 28-year-old student's half-naked body was found in a ditch on the Cairo-Alexandria highway on February 3, 2016, a week after he disappeared on the Cairo metro on January 25. items.titleIt: Regeni murdered by 'cold, methodological violence' - Rome prosecutor

items.title: Egypt, Turkey, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia FMs discuss regional security
items.description: The talks come amid regional efforts to support the implementation of a ceasefire deal signed earlier this week between the United States and Iran.
items.titleIt: Egypt, Turkey, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia FMs discuss regional security

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: Ethiopia strengthens efforts to support refugee self-reliance
items.description: In Ethiopia’s Ura refugee settlement, High Commissioner Barham Salih urged greater support for inclusive policies that enable refugees to rebuild their lives alongside hosts.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia strengthens efforts to support refugee self-reliance

items.title: Kenya labour minister accused over Russian forced recruitment
items.description: A Kenyan family's search for opportunity has ended in tragedy and sparked serious allegations against Labour Minister Alfred Mutua.
items.titleIt: Kenya labour minister accused over Russian forced recruitment

items.title: The 2026 edition of “Lab Innova for Africa ‘Luca Attanasio’” has opened in Kenya
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 22 GIU - The 2026 edition of the “Luca Attanasio” Lab Innova for Africa project for Kenya was inaugurated in Nairobi. The technical and managerial training course, promoted by the ICE Agency, includes an integrated program of assistance and training aimed at supporting the development and internationalization of Kenyan startups and innovative companies operating in the ICT sector and digital technologies. The opening day featured remarks by Marco Scoppa, Deputy Head of Mission at the Italian Embassy in Kenya; Giuseppe Manenti, Director of the ICE Office in Nairobi; and Mercy
items.titleIt: The 2026 edition of “Lab Innova for Africa ‘Luca Attanasio’” has opened in Kenya

items.title: Top UN official visits refugee inclusion project in Ethiopia
items.description: The UN High Commissioner for Refugees, Barham Salih, decribed the Makatet programme as an “important ambitious roadmap”.

items.title: Italy-Sweden Synergies in Cybersecurity Discussed in Stockholm
items.description: (ANSA) - STOCCOLMA, 23 GIU - In the context of current global challenges, all countries are now obliged to ensure secure and “non-vulnerable” technological ecosystems. Cybersecurity is therefore a strategic element in the dialogue between Italy and Sweden as well. To better and more concretely support bilateral collaboration, the event “CyberSEcurIT - Cybersecurity Synergy between Sweden and Italy: Perspectives from Academia, Industry, and Government,” dedicated specifically to cybersecurity, was held in Stockholm on June 22 and 23, 2026. Organized by research groups specializing in cybersecur
items.titleIt: Italy-Sweden Synergies in Cybersecurity Discussed in Stockholm

items.title: IMF's new Africa chief warns of Middle East conflict fallout, sees bright future for Africa
items.description: The International Monetary Fund’s new Africa chief, Zeine Zeidane, says conflict in the Middle East is creating fresh economic challenges for sub-Saharan Africa, but insists the IMF remains committed to supporting countries under pressure.
items.titleIt: IMF's new Africa chief warns of Middle East conflict fallout, sees bright future for Africa

items.title: Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions
items.description: Israel has ordered Shiite-majority communities to be emptied, accusing residents of supporting Hezbollah, while allowing Christian, Sunni and other residents to remain. That risks aggravating sectarian tensions that have been mostly dormant since Lebanon's 1982-2000 civil war.
items.titleIt: Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions

items.title: More than 3000 Malawian nationals deported in South Africa
items.description: More than 3000 Malawian nationals have been deported in South Africa after thousands protested earlier this year in Johannesburg and other parts of the country against illegal immigration that has fueled tensions between locals and foreign nationals.
items.titleIt: More than 3000 Malawian nationals deported in South Africa
